title: thymus villosus subsp. lusitanicus
intro: Thymus villosus subsp. lusitanicus, also known as Spanish thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Spanish thyme include its ability to soothe respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety levels. Spanish thyme is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, essential oils, and infusions, which can be consumed or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
